frame_label: Dress Code: Traditional = Respectable, Other = Questionable
generated_comment: Honestly yaar, some outfits just invite unnecessary attention, then later people act shocked. Indian wear looks so much more decent and elegant anyway — a saree or even a simple kurta gives such a different impression.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it links women's clothing to both respectability ('decent and elegant') and harassment ('invite unnecessary attention'). It encodes the traditional-wear superiority and subtle victim-blaming surface manifestations.
original_frame: The Makeup Paradox: Unnatural Yet Expected
persona_text: You are Priya. Age 29. Single. A woman working in an MNC in Pune. You have been told by your female manager informally that you should make more effort with your appearance because client facing roles require a certain standard of presentation. You have never worn much makeup. You are posting anonymously in a women only WhatsApp group after this conversation sharing what your manager said and how it made you feel and the impossible position women are put in Ñ judged for wearing too much and judged for wearing too little.
